result.title: Second scroll replay thread
result.summary: Switch away and confirm cached state survives.

entries.text: First thread message 1: inspect the saved viewport before switching threads. This line is intentionally verbose so the transcript grows tall enough to scroll in the replay window.
First thread message 2: the desktop client should reopen this thread without replaying a smooth descent from the top. Keep the transcript height large and stable.
First thread message 3: every message in this fixture is long on purpose. That keeps the scroll container comfortably larger than the viewport in Electron.
First thread message 4: when the renderer keeps thread state in memory, switching back should restore the exact viewport instead of treating the transcript like a cold load.
First thread message 5: we also want this fixture to explode if the app issues a third thread/read, because the replay controller would hand back the wrong transcript.
First thread message 6: cached transcript reuse matters here just as much as viewport restore. The thread heading can stay the same while the body becomes wrong if we reread.
First thread message 7: another long message keeps the transcript flowing and gives the test plenty of room to set a nonzero scrollTop before switching away.
First thread message 8: the saved viewport should be neither the top nor the bottom after restoration. It should be the same middle position the user left behind.
First thread message 9: this transcript intentionally reads like test evidence. The exact content is less important than its height and stable identity.
First thread message 10: if loading flips back to true during a cached refresh, the renderer must not rearm auto-scroll and yank the viewport to the bottom again.
First thread message 11: we keep stacking messages so the replay remains scrollable even if typography or transcript spacing shifts a little over time.
First thread message 12: the user should be able to inspect older context, click another thread, and come back without the app pretending it forgot everything.
First thread message 13: the scroll container state is part of the thread session state. Treating it as disposable creates the repeated glide shown in the bug report.
First thread message 14: once the saved viewport is captured, switching away should not overwrite it with a synthetic bottom-aligned state during a background refresh.
First thread message 15: this line keeps the replay deterministic while still being tall enough for the renderer to compute a meaningful distance from bottom.
First thread message 16: restore the viewport, preserve the cached transcript, and do not show a second thread body under the first thread title.
First thread message 17: the long fixture text also makes this scenario resilient to small changes in renderer chrome or window size across CI environments.
First thread message 18: nothing about reselecting a recently viewed thread should feel like a reload. It should feel immediate because the data is already local.
First thread message 19: after the user scrolls up, that position becomes the expected reopen target until the user scrolls somewhere else.
First thread message 20: cached transcript remains stable.

result.lastUserMessage: First thread message 19: after the user scrolls up, that position becomes the expected reopen target until the user scrolls somewhere else.
result.lastAssistantMessage: First thread message 20: cached transcript remains stable.

entries.text: Second thread message 1: this alternate transcript exists so the replay can expose an accidental reread when we switch back to the first thread.
Second thread message 2: switching away should not wipe cache.

messages.text: Second thread message 1: this alternate transcript exists so the replay can expose an accidental reread when we switch back to the first thread.
Second thread message 2: switching away should not wipe cache.
